mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so a downward trend in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because rate cuts then reflect accommodation rather than recession stress.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS is falling for 2 consecutive months, or if the signal turns negative during easing episodes in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and higher recession risk, which should raise equity risk premia and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is intended to capture labor deterioration rather than isolated noise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered months deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average 20-day return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO indicates softening industrial demand and inventory liquidation, which should weaken earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon. The mechanism fails when industrial weakness is offset by aggressive policy easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not lower after 3-month declines in INDPRO, or if the predictive sign reverses once FEDFUNDS is falling faster than 0.5 pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both weaken together, the mechanism is a broad domestic demand slowdown: retail sales point to softer consumption while housing starts confirm cooling credit-sensitive spending. That joint contraction should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined decline in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered joint weakness is followed by positive returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate becomes bearish only after it crosses a tightening threshold, because restrictive policy then compresses multiples and strains credit-sensitive sectors. Above that level, the same rate is a negative signal for 20-day S&P 500 returns; below it, the effect should fade.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 5.0% do not show lower 20-day SPX returns than periods below 5.0%, or if the threshold effect disappears in any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates that both aggregate output and consumer spending are slowing, which should lower earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The signal is strongest when both series are already below their recent quarterly trend.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to produce below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power beyond the single-series effects across a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling UNRATE during an expansion supports consumer income and margin resilience, so low unemployment should remain a positive momentum signal for equities over the next week. The effect is strongest when payroll growth is not simultaneously deteriorating.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE at or below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE episodes do not produce above-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when PAYEMS weakens at the same time.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-led demand shock transmits to equities through construction, credit, and consumer confidence, so a decline in HOUST predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. This signal fails when lower housing starts are immediately accompanied by stronger industrial production, which would indicate a sector-specific rather than economy-wide slowdown.
failure_condition: Reject if declines in HOUST are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal is not weakened when INDPRO rises on the same month.
